rulings.Case Title: PARK TERRACE AT MISSION BAY OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. 5TH & BERRY LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT A.R. SANCHEZ-COREA & ASSOCIATES INCORPORATED's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is off calendar as moot. The motion addresses the second amended complaint but plaintiff filed a third amended complaint. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131. In addition, the motion is untimely. (CCP §§ 437c(a) and 1010.6(a)(4).) The motion was served by email on August 22, 2013. Moving party did not account for the extension of the notice period by two court days for service by electronic means. (CCP § 1010.6(a)(4).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Counsel Or In The Alt To Exclude Evidence Or Argument Related To Prior Injuries, Monetary Sanctions Ot $7650.00 Against Attoneys For Deft'S And There Clients And Or Other Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CAROLYN BENGTSON's Motion To Disqualify Counsel Or In The Alternative To Exclude Evidence Or Argument Related To Prior Injuries, Monetary Sanctions Ot $7650.00 Against Attoneys For Defendants And Their Clients And Other Sanctions. Judge Goldsmith discloses that Philip M. Andersen of Philip M. Andersen & Associates has served as a Discovery Pro Tem Judge in Department 302. This will not affect Judge Goldsmith's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Denied. Neither Mr. Silver and his firm Philip M. Andersen & Associates nor Ms. Emmet and her firm Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa LLP engaged in any unethical or sanctionable behavior to warrant disqualification. The subject records were not illegally obtained and or part of sort of poisonous fruit. Ms. Emmett did not actively solicit any records from Ms. Ginn. Further, the Rhodes records from 2007 to 2009 are not privileged under California Evidence Code section 996; inquiry into the injury that Plaintiff herself as put at-issue is permissible and expressly not privileged under section 996. Mr. Silver's questions at Dr. Rhodes deposition were not improper. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW HIRSCH VS. PHOTON USA CORP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT PHOTON CONSULTING, LLC's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Granted. (CCP § 473(b).) Photon Consulting, LLC has demonstrated that it is entitled to relief from the judgment entered August 13, 2013. Photon Consulting, LLC was not aware of the date of the Labor Commissioner hearing. (Lara Decl., 11.) The Court does not consider the unpublished opinion Silva v. Magno (2011) 2011 WL 3036191 in ruling on this motion. Plaintiff's objections are overruled and Photon Consulting, LLC's objection is sustained. Photon Consulting, LLC's request for judicial notice is granted. rulings.Case Title: CINTYA GONZALEZ VS. MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CINTYA GONZALEZ's Motion To Tax Costs. The motion to tax is granted in part. The Court concludes that the motion is timely based on defendant's proof of service that indicated mail service. See CCP 12a. The Court strikes the $60 filing fee for the untimely motion to strike. The Court denies the motion as to the videotaping costs. Such costs are recoverable under CCP 1033.5(a)(3) and Plaintiff fails to show that the costs were not reasonably necessary to the litigation. See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 131. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE KILBY BLACK VS. DENNIS DOYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Morse Family Trust And Fredrick Morse'S Motion To Compel Further Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 2: Defendants MORSE FAMILY TRUST and FREDRICK MORSE'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. THE PARTIES SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS THE EXTENT TO WHICH THE MOTION MAY BE MOOT BASED ON THE RESPONSES AND VERIFICATIONS FURNISHED AFTER THE DATE OF THE MOTION. PARTIES TO APPEAR WITH PROPOSED ORDERS. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CECILIA PALMCRANTZ VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied as to summary judgment and summary adjudication. BART has not met its initial burden to demonstrate to show that the dangerous condition was not dangerous as a matter of law. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleged two dangerous conditions pertaining to the non-skid material and poor lighting conditions. BART has not addressed the allegations of poor lighting conditions. BART's safety rate is premised on an incorrect number of patrons. (Response to Plaintiff's Objections, p. 2:3-14. See UMF no. 25.) There are triable issues of fact regarding notice of a dangerous condition based on the 12/02/08 claim and whether this claim is substantially similar to the subject incident. Summary adjudication cannot lie as to part of a cause of action. (CCP § 437c(f)(1).) Plaintiff's objection to Rossen Decl., p. 1:28 - 2:1 is sustained. BART's Objection nos. 1 - 5, 7 are sustained. BART's Objection no. 6 is overruled. BART's request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CECILIA PALMCRANTZ VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. MUNI has not met its initial burden to demonstrate to show that the dangerous condition was not dangerous as a matter of law. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleged two dangerous conditions pertaining to the non-skid material and poor lighting conditions. MUNI has not addressed the allegations of poor lighting conditions. BART's safety rate is premised on an incorrect number. (See UMF no. 24.) There are triable issues of fact regarding notice of a dangerous condition based on the 12/02/08 claim and whether this claim is substantially similar to the subject incident. Although the 12/02/08 claim was filed with BART, BART and MUNI are required to equally share the investigation of all third party claims pursuant to the Joint Station Maintenance Agreement. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit G, p. 13 - 14.) Plaintiff's objection to Hammond Decl., p. 2:10-12 is sustained, and Plaintiff's objections to Hall Decl., p. 2:8-13 and 2:14-15 are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Completion Of Julie Walker'S Deposition; And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion To Compel The Completion Of Julie Walker's Deposition; And For An Award Of Sanctions. rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA MCCONNICO VS. THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petitition To Compel Arb And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Defendant's petition to compel arbitration is granted. The civil case is stayed pending completion of arbitration. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The parties formed a valid and enforceable agreement that encompasses this dispute. Plaintiff does not dispute that Plaintiff signed the Defendant's Employment, Confidential Information, Invention Assignment, and Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") on February 24, 2011. The Agreement includes an Arbitration Clause where the parties agree to arbitration all disputes "arising out of, relating to, resulting from employment ¿ including termination of employment." (See Declaration of Lynd in Support of Petition, Exh. E.) The Arbitration Clause provides for all the safeguards required by Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. The Agreement is not unenforceable on unconscionability grounds. The Agreement is not procedurally unconscionable because Plaintiff the Agreement was a "condition" of her employment. (See Lagatree v. Luce v. Forward, Hamilton & Scripps (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1122-1123.) The fact that the CAA rules are not attached is not dispositive of procedural unconscionability. (See Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) The Agreement is not substantively unconscionable as it meets the "modicum of bilaterality" standard. (See Armendariz 24 Cal.4th 83 at 117.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
